title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Jeremy Claude confesses to his wife, Francis, that he has embezzled a trust fund and needs money quickly to prevent the crime from being discovered. He expresses deep shame, believing Francis married him for stability and a life free from scandal. However, Francis laughs at his sentimentality, explaining that she married him because she fell in love with him, specifically citing his intellectual seriousness during a dinner years ago when they were wealthy.

Francis remains calm and pragmatic. She notes that her own family has a history of covering up misdeeds—such as those of her father, her cousin Charles, and her cousin Gerald—and asserts that while she is not perfectly virtuous, she is loyal. To solve the financial crisis and prevent Jeremy from going to jail, she suggests borrowing money from Rosalind, the widow of Gordon, whom she calls "the enigmatic Rosalind."

Jeremy hesitates, explaining that the funds are intended for Rosalind's living expenses. He reveals that under the law of intestacy of 1925, if Rosalind were to die, the money would pass to Gordon's nearest relatives, which would include Jeremy, Leonel, Adela, and Morris's son, Rolly. Francis realizes that this would mean the money would eventually come to them, and a chilling realization settles over the room as she processes this possibility.
